subject: Geography
title: Endemic Species
description: A short lesson to help you apply Endemic Species.

hook.text: 95% of Hawaiian land species are found nowhere else on Earth. Isolation drove unique evolution.

buildup.text: One ancestral finch in Hawaii diversified into 50+ honeycreeper species over millions of years.

discovery.text: Madagascar's lemurs descend from a single ancestor that rafted across the ocean on debris.

twist.text: Island endemics are extremely vulnerable—a single invasive species can wipe them out.

climax.text: 75% of all animal extinctions since 1500 have been island species.

punchline.text: Unique to one island. Vulnerable to everything.

quiz.question: What percentage of animal extinctions since 1500 were island species?
